old_claim.note: Public Key Infrastructure provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Digital-signature methods arise from public-key cryptography and can be defined or used without an X.509 certificate-authority infrastructure.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and Sections I-III, pp. 644-648, introduce public-key cryptosystems and a public-key authentication signature scheme without an X.509 certificate framework.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The foundational public-key paper defines digital signature authentication directly from public-key transformations and public key distribution.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The method was specified before the 1988 X.509 framework and its mathematical operation does not require certification authorities, certificate paths, or revocation services.

ontology_before: The graph reversed the historical and technical direction by making digital signatures depend on the later certificate infrastructure that itself uses signatures.
ontology_after: Digital signatures depend on cryptographic primitives, while X.509 PKI is a downstream system that uses them to certify identity-to-key bindings.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-pki-independent-identity-and-primitive-boundaries: digital_signatures no longer directly depends on public_key_infrastructure.
would_reject_if: A foundational digital-signature construction requires X.509 certification authorities, certificate paths, and revocation as part of the signing operation.
short_reason: Foundational signature schemes predate X.509 and operate on public-key primitives without requiring certificate infrastructure.
